pattern: Simulate the robot step by step using a direction vector that rotates on turn commands, advancing one unit at a time and halting against any obstacle stored in a hash set, while tracking the largest squared distance seen.
hints: Keep the heading as a small vector and rotate it left or right by swapping and negating its components on a turn command.
Move forward one cell at a time so you can stop the instant the next cell holds an obstacle; put the obstacles in a set for constant-time lookup.
After every single-unit step, update the best answer with the current squared distance from the origin; turn commands change direction but never the position.

edge_inputs.description: a turn then a forward run, no obstacles
an obstacle stops a run partway then a turn continues
obstacle at the origin blocks return but not departure
a wall of obstacles immediately ahead pins the robot
full square loop returns near the origin

stress.description: random command stream with random obstacles drawn without replacement so they are unique grid points
stress.note: obstacles kept unique; small coordinate window makes collisions with the path likely
